h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is on a straight procedural path of confirming each element of the time signature and placing the bar line, and every turn continues exactly along that path.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [traces the pen tip across the two boxes and the three tiles] '3 over 4 is visible: three beat tiles, quarter-note card in the lower box, bar line after tile three. Speed mark stays above the staff, outside the label. I can ink the 3 and the 4 now.'
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically enacts the prescribed verification gesture (tracing the pen tip) and arrives at the inking decision, which is a modest procedural action, but it does not introduce a new device or criterion beyond what the tutor explicitly modeled in T3.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so there is no reversal trigger and no adaptive mechanism change visible in any tutor turn; the tutor simply continues the same checklist-confirmation route throughout.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Because there is no pivot learner turn, there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to, and all tutor turns continue the pre-established procedural route without modification.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [traces the pen tip across the two boxes and the three tiles] '3 over 4 is visible: three beat tiles, quarter-note card in the lower box, bar line after tile three. Speed mark stays above the staff, outside the label. I can ink the 3 and the 4 now.'
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner physically enacts the prescribed verification gesture (tracing the pen tip) and arrives at the inking decision, which is a modest procedural action, but it does not introduce a new device or criterion beyond what the tutor explicitly modeled in T3.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: Because there is no pivot learner turn, there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to, and all tutor turns continue the pre-established procedural route without modification.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so there is no reversal trigger and no adaptive mechanism change visible in any tutor turn; the tutor simply continues the same checklist-confirmation route throughout.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so there is no reversal trigger and no adaptive mechanism change visible in any tutor turn; the tutor simply continues the same checklist-confirmation route throughout.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue resolving the friction arrow direction by working through the slip cue, exactly as the tutor prompted.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Slipping right relative to the table, so table-contact friction points left. Motion card stays down. Next check would be rolling right with no slip cue.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly applies the slip-cue criterion to determine friction direction and then independently projects the next test case (rolling with no slip), showing the device has been operationalized beyond the immediate prompt.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The source stays table contact. Tail stays on the lower edge. Pick the arrow card that fits this slip strip.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 introduces a concrete slip-cue strip and two arrow cards as a new physical decision device, but it does not explicitly contrast a failed prior route; it narrows and scaffolds the same contact-source logic rather than breaking from it, so only part B (new device) is legible and part A (stock-taking contrast) is absent.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake can be scored.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Slipping right relative to the table, so table-contact friction points left. Motion card stays down. Next check would be rolling right with no slip cue.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner correctly applies the slip-cue criterion to determine friction direction and then independently projects the next test case (rolling with no slip), showing the device has been operationalized beyond the immediate prompt.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake can be scored.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The source stays table contact. Tail stays on the lower edge. Pick the arrow card that fits this slip strip.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T3 introduces a concrete slip-cue strip and two arrow cards as a new physical decision device, but it does not explicitly contrast a failed prior route; it narrows and scaffolds the same contact-source logic rather than breaking from it, so only part B (new device) is legible and part A (stock-taking contrast) is absent.
